Analysis of Harmonics in the Hong Konger Power System:

Power plays an important function in modern society. The bulk of daily-use objects are connected to power in some way. People must be supplied with electricity in a safe and cost-effective way using the most advanced power technologies. Due to harmonics, the RMS of the circuit’s current flow increases, hence increasing the loss. This has resulted in several electrical grid concerns. Harmonics may influence the loads and the distribution system. Harmonics may also wreak havoc on sensitive equipment and appliances in your business or industrial building. Harmonic breakdown typically does not emerge quickly, and it might be difficult to differentiate it from normal wear and tear. 

Table of Contents

Harmonic Distortion – Definition:

If minor harmonic difficulties emerge, the power supply’s reliability might be compromised. Harmonics in the electric current and voltage produce problems with the electrical grid. An abundance of harmonics in the power supply may cause damage or malfunctions. Harmonics, or fluctuations in the shape of the current waveform, are frequently caused by nonlinear loads in electrical systems. Personal computers, powered motors, fax machines, laser printers, switch-mode power supplies (SMPS), batteries, and uninterruptible power supplies are the sources of nonlinear loads and harmonics in power systems (UPS).

Harmonic influences the operation efficiency of power systems. Using harmonic analysis to look for harmonics helps increase the dependability of your power system. As the use of power electronics equipment increases, currents that do not flow in a straight line are generated. The systems were under considerable strain, which resulted in harmonics. Equipment and devices must comply with energy efficiency standards and use less electricity. 

When did knowledge of harmonics become essential?

  • If the overall load or readings from the bus system exceed 25 percent of the total load, examine the harmonic restrictions.
  • To handle problems such as equipment damage and failures caused by harmonics.
  • As the plant grows, when and how many nonlinear loads will be introduced?
  • When adding a capacitor bank to an existing electrical system, a harmonic analysis is necessary.

When a sinusoidal waveform confronts a nonlinear demand, harmonics are produced. Harmonic analysis is frequently performed when it is essential to establish whether a system has harmonics. 

Included Among the Benefits of Removing Harmonics from the Power Supply Are:

  • Harmonic analysis reduces operating expenditures by decreasing the amount of energy lost by cables, switch gears, and safety devices. Moreover, it prevents power loss inside the power system.
  • It helps eliminate huge transformers and decreases the RMS current in the power system, resulting in lower capital expenditures. Consequently, less cable, switchgear, and busbars are required.
  • When losses are minimized and the RMS current value is dropped, there is an instantaneous improvement in business performance. Harmonics generate losses, the RMS value of the current grows, and the temperature of cables, transformers, motors, and capacitors increases. The results will be negative, and you may be forced to cancel an operation or incur monetary loss.

  • Learn as much as you can about non-linear loads and how the electric company has dealt with harmonics in the past.
  • Create a simulation of the operation of the electrical system using the ETAP program.
  • Analyze the voltage and current distortion as well as the harmonics.
  • Consider a solution if the amount of harmonic distortion is excessive.
  • After implementing the approach to remedy the issue, re-perform the harmonic analysis to check that you follow the CEC.

Analysis of Power System Load Flow, Short Circuit, and Relay Coordination

A power system analysis entails a range of technical evaluations and the use of scientific analytical concepts and methodologies to ensure that the power system at your facility is dependable, safe, and efficient in both anticipated and unanticipated situations. The objective of a power system analysis is to comprehend how a system will act in various configurations and how disturbances such as capacitor switching, the start of a large motor, and arc flash energy would affect it. A power system analysis may be required to ensure that safety equipment functions appropriately in the case of a short circuit or other failure. It is vital to investigate power systems to provide a consistent and stable supply of electricity. A well-designed power system ensures dependable operation and optimizes the plant under all conditions of operation. Outages, flaws, inefficiency, and a decline in security are all indicators of poorly developed systems.

An investigation of the average power system might include any of the following minor studies:

  1. Analysis and research on load flow
  2. On short circuits, investigation and analysis are carried out.
  3. An examination of relay-to-relay communication
  4. Analyses and Studies of Arc Flash Danger
  5. Harmonics evaluation
  6. Analysis of motion and alteration
  7. Geological investigation

This article examines the initial three investigations. and be familiar with the rules and procedures utilized in each of these investigations. 

Table of Contents

Investigation and Analysis of Load Flow:

A load flow study is done to determine the direction of electricity flow inside an electrical network. It aids in evaluating the power system’s efficiency, the quantity of active and reactive power generated, losses, voltage, current, and power factor.

Three components comprised the load flow analysis:

  • Modelling of power system networks and components
  • Producing load flow equations by a certain procedure.
  • Use mathematics to solve the equations expressing the load-flow connection.

There are three ways to obtain power system information:

  • The Gauss-Seidel System is a popular data analysis tool. This approach has the advantages of being user-friendly, utilizing little computer power, and completing the task rapidly. However, further phases are required since the convergence is taking so long. The number of iterations increases as the number of buses increases.
  • The more sophisticated Newton-Raphson approach employs quadratic convergence, which is useful in difficult-to-understand circumstances. This approach utilizes fewer computing resources since convergence requires fewer iterations. It is more precise because it is less affected by variables such as regulating transformers and bus selection slack. Programming may be difficult and requires a significant amount of machine memory.
  • Fast Decoupled Load Flow System is a further method for doing load flow analysis (FDLF). The primary advantage of this strategy is that it utilises less computer memory. Since it can do computations five times quicker than the Newton-Raphson technique, it is commonly used to control the power grid in real time. This program may only be utilized under conditions. Therefore, it is more difficult to change it in the power business to account for flow or system protection.

Investigation and Examination of Short Circuits:

In a short circuit analysis, one or more of the following types of short circuits will be examined:

  • When two phases short out simultaneously, a line-to-line fault occurs.
  • A single line-to-ground failure impacts only one ground phase.
  • A double line-to-ground fault arises from the simultaneous shorting of both phases to the ground.
  • There is a short between all three phases in a three-phase fault.

Once the nature of the problem has been identified, it will be easy to create a one-line diagram of the power distribution system. Calculate short circuits using the produced single-line diagram, impedance diagram, and numerical values for the utility source, transformer, and conductor in relation to the potential voltage that each component may consume. The full-load amps, transformer multiplier, and short circuit current may be measured. To ensure that the power distribution system has the appropriate safety measures in place at various locations to prevent accidents and downtime, it is essential to compare the findings with the equipment ratings. 

Coordination Relay Analysis:

Relay coordination is an essential part of power system safety design because coordination systems make sure that relays operate quickly, dependably, and selectively to isolate the issue. Calculating the number of overcurrent protection devices needed in a power system involves coordination analysis. It also aids in determining the specifications, configurations, and dimensions necessary to strike a balance between protecting equipment and placing constraints on the system’s functionality. For data collecting, software like ETAP, which mimics the power system, is utilized. Following a short circuit investigation, the fault currents at every electrical site are measured. The protective devices are then chosen and installed to mitigate the impact of system equipment failures. The time-current characteristic curves of the protective device are monitored and compared to localized areas of inefficient coordination. Adjustments are done as necessary to ensure that protective mechanisms function uniformly and selectively. 

Listed Below Are Some Advantages of Power System Analysis:

  • The infrastructure of electricity has been upgraded.
  • Use the proper instruments with the appropriate power rating.
  • Electrical hazards will become safer and less hazardous.
  • Respect all applicable regulations and electrical installation standards.
  • A list of suggestions for improving the performance and dependability of the system.
  • A record of the current operation of the power system has been created for future inspections.

CareLabs provides Load Flow analysis, Short Circuit analysis, and Relay coordination analysis to monitor the voltage at various buses as well as the actual and reactive power flow between buses, analyze the system under various fault conditions, and determine the system’s ability to handle small and momentous changes. Please contact us or submit a price request if your company needs an inspection of the electrical system at its site. Within 24 hours, a member of our customer care team will contact you.

CareLabs provides priced electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services to businesses of all sizes. To ensure that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and comprehensive, certified professionals will analyze arc flash hazards, employee training, documentation, and PPE (Personal Protection Equipment) requirements. CareLabs can instantly identify arc flash threats and reduce the impact of harmonic distortions to safeguard the equipment. ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software is utilized to investigate and evaluate Arc flashes. The amount of light and heat generated by an arc fault, also known as an arc flash. Something occurs when current flows across an air gap between conductors. The arc flash ionizes the atmosphere and produces a substantial amount of heat and light (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). As part of an arc flash analysis, the electrical installations of a facility are studied and assessed to determine the incident energy available for each piece of electrical equipment. Arc flash studies enhance workplace safety by defining criteria for qualified electricians to follow when operating equipment and circuits. Included as well are a short circuit and coordination analysis, new electrical one-line schematics, and equipment placements. 

Objectives of Arc Flash Research:

  • Determine how much damage was inflicted by the incident energy discharged by an arc flash fault.
  • modifying the settings of protective equipment to minimize exposure to incident energy
  • Describe the risks posed by arc flash safety regulations.
  • Respect the country’s specific laws and regulations.
  • Determine the type of PPE being used at work.

The Adverse Consequences of Arc Flash:

  • Arcs produce severe facial, ocular, and skin burns.
  • Inhaling gases and heated particles can severely burn the throat and lungs.
  • Flying debris and unprotected parts cause injury.

Arc flash studies are essential for reducing hazards and enhancing the safety of electrical employees and the entire organization. Arc flash investigations assist in identifying electrical hazard levels and implementing the appropriate safety measures to reduce the risk of accidents and burns. All utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial companies employing important electrical components such as electrical disconnects, motor controllers, panel boards, and switches are required to undertake arc flash studies. 

Advantages of Arc Flash Study :

  • Increased human and mechanical safety
  • Lower medical and legal service expenses
  • Compliance with legal and safety regulations
  • Fewer rule violations and penalties.

Check and Test the Performance of Electric Motors According to Hong Konger Rules

Globally, industrial electric motors are utilized to power a vast array of applications. The International Energy Agency (IEA) estimates that the automobile industry consumes up to 70% of all industrial energy, 35% of energy utilized in the commercial and service sectors, and 45% of all produced power. Electric motors are vital to a variety of industrial processes, as each hour of downtime caused by a motor failure costs thousands of dollars. They are the primary cause of greenhouse gas emissions from power plants and their detrimental consequences on the environment. 

In addition, they are responsible for the significant increase in power usage in emerging nations. Globally, advances in industrial motor energy efficiency have the potential to enhance the economy by 20% to 30%, with most payback times of less than three years. Electric motors are responsible for approximately 15%, or 4.3 billion tons, of the world’s yearly CO2 emissions, which total 26 billion tons. Increasing energy efficiency is one of the most cost-effective and low-risk solutions to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and halt the increase in energy consumption.

Utilize a motor that is in good condition since:

  • Reduced operational expenses
  • A farther, colder, and more tranquil distance.
  • Motors with increased efficiency and durability
  • Lower emissions of greenhouse gases into the atmosphere.

Table of Contents

Starting a Motor:

Typically, electric motors do not convert 100 percent of the electricity they receive into mechanical energy. Some energy is wasted due to friction, windage, and losses in the stator, rotor, and magnetic core. These losses are reducing the performance of the motor. Considering how much energy is utilized and how electric motors are utilized in industry, it is evident why and how crucial it is to analyze energy efficiency and establish basic energy performance standards. How precisely efficiency and motor loss are determined depends on the testing method chosen. No testing procedure is employed worldwide across all industries. Although the concept is simple, it may be difficult to quantify and verify the energy efficiency of a motor using various standards.

Methods for Measuring Effectiveness That Are Widely Employed:

In industrial environments, the following testing procedures are commonly mentioned: 

IEEE (Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers) 112-2004 is a typical method for testing multiphase motors and generators. Tests, “Methods for Calculating the Efficiency and Losses of Rotating Electrical Equipment,” International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) Publication 60034-2-1, 2014. JEC 37 was developed by the Japanese Electrotechnical Committee as the standard for induction devices. IEEE Standard 112-2004 IEEE Standard 112-2004 

This approach for determining the efficiency of energy usage employs 10 measures. To establish what is most essential: 

  • Simple test of inputs and outputs
  • Testing with input and output that are loss-separated.
  • Testing two interconnected devices back-to-back.
  • The load losses are computed utilizing the smoothed residual losses.
  • The “Eh-star” strategy

IEC standard testing is available in three distinct formats: 

  • Determining the input and output power of a machine.
  • Monitoring the power transfer between two series-connected devices.
  • The losses of a single machine were tallied.

The increased load losses have no effect on the test techniques of the Japanese JEC standard 37. CareLabs provides a range of electrical installation services, including safety inspection, testing, calibration, and certification, in addition to comprehensive investigation and analysis of harmonics. Your electrical safety program will be reviewed by trained personnel to ensure that it is current, compliant, and comprehensive. In addition, these experts will evaluate harmonic threats, training materials, paperwork, and personal protection equipment (PPE). To ensure the equipment is running safely, CareLabs can detect harmonic distortions early on and take the necessary measures to mitigate their effect. Harmonic voltages and currents could degrade the performance of an electrical system. They may degrade the quality of the power and are inefficient heaters. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical approach for predicting resonances and degrees of harmonic distortion. 

Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical technique that evaluates data to anticipate potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels. Harmonic frequencies are introduced into the electrical system by nonlinear switching equipment, such as AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, and computers, among others. When harmonic voltage and current levels are high, equipment failure is likely. Therefore, it is essential to study and analyze harmonics when appropriate. The current harmonic limit for 415-volt systems is 5%, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics. 

Harmonic Distortion Effects:

  • It is responsible for current flow and high voltage.
  • Severe voltage distortion causes equipment failure and destruction
  • A rise in the quantity of internal energy in components results in higher fragility and a shorter life duration.
  • Errors in measurement cause branch circuit breakers to trip.
  • Systems of wiring and distribution exploding and burning
  • Generator failure
  • Fall in system power factor
  • The installation and utility costs are expensive.
  • Reduction in efficiency
  • Driving aimlessly and taking unnecessary breaks are not required.
  • Larger engines are employed

Harmonic issues are uncommon, but when they do occur, they tend to reduce the power system’s dependability, efficiency, and quality. Therefore, harmony must be explored and evaluated.

  • Protection against voltage and current harmonics
  • Frequency fluctuations restrict out-of-phase voltage and current.
  • Improve power factor analysis and maintain a value close to 1.
  • Regulates voltage peaks and troughs

With the assistance of harmonic investigation and analysis, it will be simpler to determine what may or may not be creating power quality issues. This will aid in preventing harmonics-related design issues and reduce the likelihood of undesirable outcomes. 

Harmonic Study and Analysis Workflow at CareLabs:

  • On the single-line schematic of an electrical circuit, call attention to the long medium-voltage cables, large capacitor banks, and nonlinear loads.
  • Identify the connection (PCC)
  • Determine which system buses in the facility are susceptible to harmonic distortions.
  • Collect harmonic information for all nonlinear loads.
  • Obtain vital information on the maximum and minimum short circuit fault levels, as well as the harmonic levels.
  • ETAP is used to analyze the electrical network for harmonics.
  • Determine the overall and individual voltage and current wave distortion values of the bus and PCC.
  • Create the frequency spectrum of the harmonics.
  • If harmonic distortion levels are above the acceptable threshold, a remedy is proposed and implemented.
  • Rerun the analysis once the countermeasure has been implemented.

Advantages of CareLabs's offerings:

If background harmonics have previously been measured, higher ratings are applied in the second stage.

Hong Konger Arc Flash Analysis: A Comprehensive Guide

When a gas is exposed to a voltage greater than it can block, electrical arcs form. The arc begins to develop when the voltage is high enough to ionize the air between the conductors. When a gas is ionized, it becomes an excellent conductor, allowing electricity to flow and producing an “arc flash.” A fault arc is an unintended electrical discharge.

  • Extremely elevated temperatures cause an arc flash, which creates dazzling light and a loud roar.
  • An arc blast is a high-pressure wave that may propel molten metal and metal fragments into the air.
  • Poisonous vapors

Arc flash dangers have the capacity to cause severe damage to companies and electrical infrastructure. Listed below are some of the business losses caused by arc flash hazards.

  • Additional expenses associated with medical treatment, therapy, workers’ compensation, accident investigation, and production downtime.
  • Included in the costs associated with litigation are court fees, penalties, insurance premiums, repair expenses, and the cost of recruiting a new employee.
  • A negative impact on the name and reputation of the firm.
  • The loss of economic potential caused by a history of accidents, which might result in the revocation of an electrical license.

Table of Contents

Why Do Arc Flashes Occur?

  • Insulating damage to the conductor.
  • Visible elements of a person’s body
  • Human irresponsibility
  • Poorly maintained switches and circuit breakers
  • The equipment is located close to the water.
  • Disconnect any panels that are in the way.
  • Elevated-voltage conductors
  • Static electricity discharges defective equipment and tools.

Arc Flash Assessment Technique:

Arc flashes can occur anywhere high voltage or low voltage with a high current is switched. 

Determine the probability of an arc flash:
  • Perform an arc flash/fault analysis or study, including data collecting, software modelling of the power system, analysis of short circuits, examination of interactions between overcurrent protection devices, analysis of arc flashes, and assessment of arc flashes.
  • Calculating the likelihood that a worker may be injured by an arc flash
Control the risk:
  • Employees are less likely to get arc flash accidents if they only handle equipment that is turned off.
  • Manual systems would be inferior to automated ones.
  • Install physical barriers between personnel and machinery to safeguard their safety.
Control of processes by engineers:
  • Reduce the fault current to lessen the incident energy.
  • Systems for arc flash detection and prevention
  • Modifications to the management of switches and remotes or switchboard upgrades
  • Circuit breakers that are immobile
  • Ensure that the bus bars are insulated.
  • Purchase replacement fuses for any that have blown.
Administratively imposed safeguards:
  • Determining the outside border of an arc by labelling plants, switch rooms, and other crucial locations where energy levels vary.
  • Arc flash diagrams and research notes.
  • Implementing security principles and methods to reduce exposure
  • Using PPE (Personal Protection Equipment) levels and incident energy
Equipment for personal protection (PPE):
  • Even though PPE is the least effective kind of protection, it can minimize the severity of an accident, but it cannot prevent one. The type of personal protection equipment (PPE) utilized varies on the level of hazard. 

CareLabs can assist you with any issues linked to arc flash. Professionals with in-depth training and understanding can identify arc flash dangers and implement the most effective mitigation and management strategies to protect your workers and property.

  • Integrated electrical systems save money and time.
  • Enhance the safety of the workplace.
  • Provide choices for emergency situations.
  • Increase the margins of safety
  • Automate the creation of arc flash labels and work permits to save time.
  • Avoid potential penalties and legal expenses.

How Can the Quality and Dependability of Commercial Motors Be Evaluated?

Increasing energy efficiency is the cleanest, cheapest, and most efficient approach to offer dependable electricity. Motors consume about 46% of all electricity and 69% of all energy consumed in the industrial sector. Utilization of energy is one of the most essential issues faced by many enterprises. Poor architecture and wasteful power use are the primary issues that must be addressed. Poor design and incorrect use are the primary causes of rising energy costs and difficulty in meeting the increased demand for power. Most motors operate between 50 and 100 percent of their rated load, but they are most efficient around 75 percent of their rated load, and efficiency begins to decline as a motor ages. At 50% load, an electric motor’s efficiency immediately begins to decrease. 

The performance of a motor is governed by four factors:

  • Poor power quality has a direct impact on a motor’s performance, since transients, imbalances, sags, swells, and harmonics may all severely damage a motor or, in extreme cases, cause it to cease functioning entirely.
  • When a motor produces rotational force to move a mechanical load at the rated speed of the spinning shaft, torque is produced. Since the electric motor operates at the specified frequency and torque speed, it is dependable. The measurement of torque offers information into the motor’s efficiency.
  • The following information describes the electric motor’s operation and what to anticipate from it: A motor’s mechanical overwork places stress on its connection, bearings, and insulation. This decreases the motor’s efficiency and raises the probability that it may malfunction or fail before its planned lifespan.
  • In conclusion, the efficiency of a motor has a direct impact on the bottom line. Collecting baseline data is the initial phase in predictive and preventative maintenance.

75% of industrial motor failures annually result in a 1-to-6-day shutdown of operations. After a warning, 36% of motor failures occurred within a day, and 90% occurred within a month. To prevent motor failure and operating issues by regularly monitoring, analysing, inspecting, and auditing your electric motors, you may prevent issues and increase their efficiency and dependability.

An induction motor’s efficiency over a 53-year period by 68%. (1960 to 2013). The efficiency of electric induction motors will increase if annual losses are decreased. Over time, the energy consumption of an induction motor has decreased (data of 45 kW, 4p design motor). How Can the Quality and Dependability of Commercial Motors Be Evaluated?

Increasing energy efficiency is the cleanest, cheapest, and most efficient approach to offer dependable electricity. Motors consume about 46% of all electricity and 69% of all energy consumed in the industrial sector. Utilization of energy is one of the most essential issues faced by many enterprises. Poor architecture and wasteful power use are the primary issues that must be addressed. Poor design and incorrect use are the primary causes of rising energy costs and difficulty in meeting the increased demand for power. Most motors operate between 50 and 100 percent of their rated load, but they are most efficient around 75 percent of their rated load, and efficiency begins to decline as a motor ages. At 50% load, an electric motor’s efficiency immediately begins to decrease.

Poor power quality has a direct impact on a motor’s performance, since transients, imbalances, sags, swells, and harmonics may all severely damage a motor or, in extreme cases, cause it to cease functioning entirely.
When a motor produces rotational force to move a mechanical load at the rated speed of the spinning shaft, torque is produced. Since the electric motor operates at the specified frequency and torque speed, it is dependable. The measurement of torque offers information into the motor’s efficiency.
The following information describes the electric motor’s operation and what to anticipate from it: A motor’s mechanical overwork places stress on its connection, bearings, and insulation. This decreases the motor’s efficiency and raises the probability that it may malfunction or fail before its planned lifespan.
In conclusion, the efficiency of a motor has a direct impact on the bottom line. Collecting baseline data is the initial phase in predictive and preventative maintenance.

How to Conduct a Power Quality Analysis in Hong Kong?

The phrase “power quality” refers to the efficiency with which a piece of equipment utilizes the given energy. Power quality issues result in excessive energy consumption, expensive energy bills, high maintenance costs, rapid economic repercussions, lost productivity, downtime, irreplaceable resources, and unstable or damaged equipment. Any organization should consider energy management, and any energy management program should consider power quality. CareLabs provides a variety of services that address several energy-related issues. We provide services for studying and analysing power quality based on our industry knowledge. 

Concerns Regarding the Quality of the Power:

Power supply quality is a more sophisticated and delicate metric than dependability. It is irrelevant if there is sufficient supply. Rather, the question is whether the supply can be utilized. 

Changes in power quality may be categorized into two categories:
Revolutions:

If the voltage or current are off, a disruption may occur. When the peak value of a transient voltage exceeds a certain threshold, it is possible to compute it. Voltage fluctuations such as dips, swells, and interruptions can be observed when the RMS variation surpasses a specific threshold.  

Variations from the steady state:

Fluctuations in the RMS voltage and the distortion of harmonic waves are two instances of steady-state variations. Voltage and current must be monitored to monitor these changes over time. These statistics give the most precise illustration of voltage distortion throughout time. Other approaches, such as the average distortion level, which has a 95% chance of not being surpassed, may then be used to analyze it. 

Table of Contents

Several variables impact supplier quality, including:
  • Constant voltage
  • How often it is made accessible
  • The voltage lowers
  • Spikes in voltage
  • Changes in voltage
  • Cohesive changes
  • Estimation of radio frequency

Most of these items are difficult to locate and see. Typically, specialized testing equipment is required, thus only skilled technicians or engineers should measure them. 

Constant Voltage:

As a result of load and other system events, the steady-state voltage levels of an electrical system are constantly fluctuating. Voltage levels often decrease as the load increases. Like this, when a load is removed, the voltage level rises. 

Unbalanced Voltage:

Either the phase angle or the three-phase supply value is different. It can also be caused by unbalanced loads and impedances in the circuit. It increases heat, damages machinery, and decreases output.

Businesses in Hong Kong may utilize the Motor start research and analysis service provided by CareLabs. The primary objective of our motor start analysis service is to determine what peculiar occurrences occur when a large motor is started. There are more factors to consider before starting an electric motor in addition to ensuring that it is correctly connected to the line voltage. Unwanted tripping, high nominal currents, and fading or flickering lights are all indications that an electrical system needs maintenance. CareLabs will dispatch qualified staff to your location to collect both current and historical data on the motor installation. Using the gathered data and manufacturer’s information, calculations are then performed to estimate what will occur in the future and to devise solutions to mitigate any potential adverse consequences. 

Using static and dynamic modelling, one may investigate motor-starting techniques. We focus mostly on dynamic motor starting research that attempts to simulate motor starting conditions such as:

  • To simulate the load flow solution of an electric motor, its measured starting impedance is employed.
  • The voltage drop method is used to calculate the current in a motor when it is turned on for the first time, whereas the short-circuit method is used to compute the current under pre-fault short circuit conditions.
  • When an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is available, the dynamic model of the electric motor may be included into a typical transient stability technique to examine the consequences of the motor beginning.
  • In the lack of a comprehensive dynamic model of the electric motor, transient stability simulations may be employed.

Hong Kong Must Perform an Examination of Motor:

Modern industrial systems require motors, and the size of electric motors is expanding tremendously. If you switch on a large motor that is linked to the line, it might pose a threat to the electric motor, any local loads, and distant buses. For an electric motor to start, the terminal voltage must be at least 80% of the rated voltage or greater. Since the square of a motor’s terminal voltage is directly proportional to its load torque, voltage differential quickly influences the motor’s load torque characteristic.

If there is a significant voltage drop, the operating machines may endure greater stress than their “breakdown torque,” causing them to slow down or halt. Other types of loads, including lighting loads, delicate control systems, and electrical equipment, are also affected by the voltage drop. In addition, high beginning current reduces terminal voltage, necessitating an increase in current from all systems that service a steady load. Broken relays for low voltages have also been discovered. 

When Is a Motor Start Analysis Required?

  • Prior to or at the time of purchasing a large engine.
  • If there is no generator and the motor rating exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
  • If the generator is the only source of power for the system and the motor rating exceeds 10 to 15 percent of the generator’s KVA capacity, the generator should be replaced.
  • A motor start analysis may be required if many motors start simultaneously for an industrial operation.
  • Voltage drop issues are exacerbated when a motor with a lot of power is started, because smaller power supply often has less power. It may be feasible to gain a better understanding of the output of tiny generator-powered devices by examining how motors start.

An Analysis of the Benefits of Motor Start:

Analyses of how an engine begins Choose a motor or starting method with the optimal speed-torque characteristics for the task at hand. To prevent exceeding the maximum permitted torque of the apparatus, certain loads must be increased while being continuously monitored. A motor start analysis may always be used to examine the motor shaft. Consequently, it aids in determining how much torque the machinery can withstand. 

It is vital to do research on motors, beginning to determine how starting affects the voltage of the machine and if an electric motor can start and accelerate rapidly. CareLabs provides motor start tests conducted by trained experts to assist you evaluate the performance of your electric motor. In addition to Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau Island, we offer motor start analysis services in all major cities. Get in contact with us as soon as possible to organize your motor starting study or to acquire a price.

If there is a significant voltage drop, the operating machines may endure greater stress than their “breakdown torque,” causing them to slow down or halt. Other types of loads, including lighting loads, delicate control systems, and electrical equipment, are also affected by the voltage drop. In addition, high beginning current reduces terminal voltage, necessitating an increase in current from all systems that service a steady load. Broken relays for low voltages have also been discovered. 

When Is a Motor Start Analysis Required?
Prior to or at the time of purchasing a large engine.
If there is no generator and the motor rating exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
If the generator is the only source of power for the system and the motor rating exceeds 10 to 15 percent of the generator’s KVA capacity, the generator should be replaced.
A motor start analysis may be required if many motors start simultaneously for an industrial operation.
Voltage drop issues are exacerbated when a motor with a lot of power is started, because smaller power supply often has less power. It may be feasible to gain a better understanding of the output of tiny generator-powered devices by examining how motors start.
An Analysis of the Benefits of Motor Start:

Analyses of how an engine begins Choose a motor or starting method with the optimal speed-torque characteristics for the task at hand. To prevent exceeding the maximum permitted torque of the apparatus, certain loads must be increased while being continuously monitored. A motor start analysis may always be used to examine the motor shaft. Consequently, it aids in determining how much torque the machinery can withstand. 

It is vital to do research on motors, beginning to determine how starting affects the voltage of the machine and if an electric motor can start and accelerate rapidly. CareLabs examines the quality of the power for companies around Honk Kong. Our objective is to prevent electrical equipment, appliances, and installations from malfunctioning due to fluctuations in the power supply. Numerous organizations and a rising number of individual customers utilize data gathering, monitoring, and measurement equipment that is particularly sensitive to fluctuations in the power supply. Most of the electrical equipment is designed to accommodate specific supply changes, but few are designed to accommodate the wide range of voltage swings typical with distributed power. CareLabs provides the expertise and experience required to assist you with equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning difficulties.

The Purpose of Power Quality Analysis Is To:

  • Locating the load’s and the electrical system’s issues’ origin.
  • Utilizing the workplace to enhance simulation methodologies for discovering answers to power quality issues.
  • Assessing the performance and efficiency of heavy equipment
  • Enhance the machine’s overall performance and power factor.
  • Try not to exert excessive energy.
  • To prevent things from breaking down and halting.
  • Removing power-degrading problems such as transients, harmonics, and spikes.
  • Prevent voltage and frequency spikes as well as PLC stalling.
  • To prevent problems such as capacitor explosions, transformer overheating, and nuisance tripping.
  • Significantly reduces your power expenditure.
  • Extend the life of the electrical network and its components.
  • Determine if the electrical equipment can resist alterations.

Hong Kong Should Evaluate Its Energy Supply:

All electrical networks endure power outages, which occur more often when storms, accidents, or other natural catastrophes destroy power lines. Variations in power supply are quite natural. There are three sorts of alterations, interruptions, and distortions that affect the ability of an electrical network to transmit power:

1. A frequency deviation occurs when a frequency strays from its native frequency range.

2.Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage’s amplitude exceeds its normal range, but its shape stays unchanged.

2.1 Voltage spikes and dips, both lasting up to a minute, are examples of short-term fluctuations.

2.2 Under voltages and excess voltages are examples of persistent fluctuations.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage: This problem causes the equipment to overheat when the load is not distributed evenly throughout the phases.

2.4 Difference in voltage between neutral and earth

3. Disturbances caused by distortions that occur when the voltage contour is not even or smooth.

3.1 Due to harmonics and inter harmonics, there are resonance issues, overheating, power loss, and power loss in electronic devices.

3.2 Transients with durations of less than one-tenth of a second have the potential to damage switch contact surfaces, reduce system performance, and accelerate the failure of motor drives and control.

What Does Power Quality Analysis Comprise?

To identify various power quality concerns, several methods must be employed. Among these tactics are the following:

  • Utilizing computer algorithms and reduction approaches, harmonic analysis identifies and anticipates probable harmonic difficulties.
  • This examination will uncover surges and transients.
  • Study of Voltage Dips and Climbs This analysis would monitor the voltage’s value, trajectory, and short-term dips and climbs.
  • This study will identify the optimal reactive power at the distribution and load ends.
  • This analysis would examine and coordinate captive power to satisfy demand while minimizing fuel and energy surcharges.
  • This research determines the magnitudes of power flows, power factors, voltage levels, and losses in system branches depending on the operating circumstances.

To enhance the dependability and quality of its clients’ electrical systems, CareLabs provides the finest service possible. In addition to Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau Island, we provide power quality diagnostic services in every other major city. CareLabs provides electrical installation services including power quality analysis studies, electrical safety inspections, simulations of electrical system design, as well as research and analysis of power systems. Our team of electrical professionals conducts load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies utilizing ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), the industry’s premier power system transient analysis tool. It enables us to collaborate directly with our clients to create and implement the most effective power compensation and solutions. 

Why Would an Examination of the Electricity System Be Conducted?

Every industrial or commercial structure must have a continuous and dependable power source. Even a little power loss or failure can cause significant difficulties, such as extended downtime, damage to vital equipment, and repair costs. On a regular basis, your building’s power system might undergo an examination to ensure that it is dependable, safe, and efficient under normal and abnormal conditions. A complete study is necessary at each stage of the design, installation, and operation of a power system. 

Using the ETAP software tool, we will examine and analyze the power system to gain an understanding of its regular and emergency operation. It is a compendium of engineering studies that examines the transient, dynamic, and static responses of an electric power system. To evaluate the status of your institution’s electrical system, many studies may be conducted. It might be included in a report on the power system’s study. Here are some examples of this type:

An Analysis of Load Circulation:

By conducting load flow studies to discover the voltages and currents in various network segments, you may select the appropriate rated equipment. Using ETAP’s built-in load flow function, you can determine a power system’s voltages and currents, as well as the amount of power being consumed and its power factor. 

Studies of Short Circuits:

It is vital to conduct short-circuit studies to comprehend how a problem impacts the functioning of a power system. Manually investigating short circuits takes a significant amount of time; software like ETAP that can perform the task accurately is far faster. ETAP is utilized to simulate system protection devices to assess the influence of 3-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures on distribution systems. 

Temporal Analysis:

Studies of transients identify the magnitude and nature of overvoltage and current surges caused by lightning strikes and switching operations. You may select how much insulation and surge protection you need based on the results.

This research aims to ensure that the security components of your system are functioning properly. It verifies that they are the correct size and fit the object they are protecting.

The Significance of Arc Flash Hazard Analysis and Mitigation Techniques

Electrical arcs may emerge when a gas is subjected to a voltage greater than its ability to block. An arc will form when the voltage is high enough to ionize the area between the conductors. Air transforms into a superb electrical conductor when ions are introduced. Ionization of the air creates a current that warms and illuminates it. As a result of this heat, ionized air rises, creating an arch-shaped current. Due to this, the phenomenon is referred described as an arch current. An arc flash is capable of producing temperatures of up to 20,000 degrees Celsius.

Arc flash hazards pose a threat to both humans and property. Arc in flash offers a variety of commercial benefits, including:

  • Direct expenses include payment for medical treatment, therapy, worker’s compensation, the cost of investigating the incident, and lost productivity.
  • Indirect expenses include legal and judgement fees, court fees, penalties, insurance premiums, repair costs, and the cost of recruiting a new employee.
  • When unpleasant events occur, your reputation suffers, obtaining a job becomes more difficult, and you may even face criminal charges.
  • Consumers prefer to avoid businesses with a high accident rate, which can lead to lost sales and the loss of a contractor’s license.
  • Arcing errors Flash has a propensity to stick in people’s minds, which may result in missed opportunities and wasted funds.

Table of Contents

Where Do Arc Flash Dangers Originate?

  • Utilizing the incorrect surface for the probe inappropriate installation, work procedures, and equipment usage of inferior electrical components
  • Equipment holes or insulation damage
  • Eliminate panel interference
  • Dust on electrical equipment or corrosion
  • Inadequate upkeep of switches and circuit breakers
  • Visible live components or frayed connections to either high-voltage lines or a constant power supply are hazardous.
  • Moisture in electrical equipment

Methods for Reducing the Risk of Arc Flashes:

Eliminating electrical components:

Potential dangers are minimized to the greatest degree possible. Work should not be performed on live equipment, and testing the equipment after it has been de-energized or re-energized necessitates special caution. Remote tracking is used to keep circuit breakers away from arc flash boundaries because they represent a risk to the user. 

Utilize technology without taking risks:

To determine the dangers associated with short circuits and the interaction between protective devices, arc flash analysis and power system studies are conducted. Use low-risk options, such as remote rack equipment, to safeguard people and property. 

Changes must be made to the electrical and control systems:

Depending on the amount of threat posed by the arc flash, ensure that the appropriate level of PPE (personal protective equipment) is available. Modifications are being made to engineering tools and procedures to make them safer. Installing circuit breakers and energy distribution systems restricts the quantity of energy that may go through. 

Diminish the fault current that is accessible:

Why Are Harmonic Analysis and Research Crucial for Hong Konger Businesses?

Harmonics are vital to a power system because they alter the quality of the electricity and how things function. Overheating produced by high harmonic levels may impair the lifespan of the equipment. Harmonics modify the pure sine wave generated by a perfect power source. Harmonic distortion complicates the operation and production of enterprises. The following are some commercial effects of harmonic distortion.

  • Harmonics result in greater energy loss from components (Joule effect).
  • When harmonics are present, the quantity of power subscribed to grows. As a result, costs rise, and consumer services become more expensive.
  • Large apparatus with insufficient power source
  • When the amount of distortion exceeds 10%, the lifespan of many components is reduced, resulting in a decline in output.
  • Harmonics-caused peak currents trip obsolete equipment, resulting in output losses and a significant cost for the time required to bring the system back online.

Table of Contents

Harmonics Result In:

Harmonics are created by loads that draw electricity in brief, sudden bursts rather than constantly. These pulses produce harmonic currents to flow as distorted current waves throughout the power system. Harmonics reduce the dependability and efficiency of industrial electricity. Up to 15% of nonlinear loads may be safely managed by most organizations’ electrical systems. If nonlinear loads account for more than 15% of the overall load, harmonic distortions may be hazardous. Therefore, it is vital to monitor harmonic levels to ensure they do not become excessive. Harmonic research and analysis are important for monitoring potential risks and causes. By doing so, it may be simpler to avoid harmonic-related issues at the design phase and less probable that undesirable outcomes would occur.

Analysis of Harmonics Technique:

Even if harmonic issues are unlikely, they must be analyzed immediately to ensure they are not grave. Here are the harmonic level’s stages:

  • Obtaining a one-line electrical system diagram and identifying the available nonlinear loads is the initial stage.
  • The emphasis of the graphic is on the location of the common connection (PCC).
  • In system buses, emphasis is concentrated on distortion-prone plants.
  • All information on harmonics has been gathered.
  • Ask your utility provider for the PCC values that are in place. This contains the current and voltage harmonics allowed as well as the maximum and minimum short circuit fault levels.
  • We do a simulated harmonic analysis using open-source software.
  • Harmonically analyze the electrical system.
  • Find the bus and PCC’s discrete and overall discrete current and voltage aberrations. It is necessary to generate a spectrum picture of the harmonic frequencies.
  • Select the most effective harmonics control measures. Harmonic distortion exceeds acceptable levels.
  • After implementing remedial measures, do a harmonic analysis.

Why Load Flow and Short Circuit Analysis Are Important for a Hong Konger Business?

We are all aware of the significance of electricity to our everyday life. For effective energy generation, transmission, and distribution, the system must be in equilibrium. Load flow is the most essential topic to know when undertaking an examination of any system’s power system. Load flow analysis reveals the voltage of the entire system, the quantity of active and reactive power generated and consumed, and the line losses. By doing a short circuit analysis, you may assess if your power system can manage the potential of power component failure. 

A short circuit may have serious effects. It might result in overheating, a fire, or even an explosion if the circuit is damaged. Short circuits and load flow can severely reduce the efficiency of a machine. This blog article explains in detail how to discover problems such as load flow and short circuits using simulation approaches. We use specialized software referred to as the Electrical Transient Analyzer Program to investigate load flow and short circuit issues (ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program)). Using ETAP data, a comprehensive analysis of load flow and short circuit was done. 

Why are short circuit and load flow studies so important for Hong Kong businesses? 

In every business, the current electrical infrastructure is problematic. Short circuits and load flow were the two most common problems. A load flow and short circuit study is the best technique to determine what is causing these load flow and short circuit issues. 

Table of Contents

Why Does a Short Circuit Occur?

  • A broken wire or insufficient insulation may result in a short circuit or energy surge if current passes via the neutral line.
  • Poor connection A circuit requires a solid connection. Allowing electricity to flow via the neutral or grounded conductor might produce a short circuit if a wire is loose.
  • Use a damaged outlet: If anything is connected to a damaged outlet, short circuits may develop.

In contrast, a transient electrical system surge typically causes load flow. Load flow analysis is a technique used by professionals at CareLabs to solve problems, particularly those involving load flow. Short circuit analysis resolves difficulties with short circuits. CareLabs has specialists that can do load flow and short circuit analysis to determine what is wrong and assist you in fixing it.

Why Load Flow and Short Circuit Analysis Are Important for a Hong Konger Business?

We are all aware of the significance of electricity to our everyday life. For effective energy generation, transmission, and distribution, the system must be in equilibrium. Load flow is the most essential topic to know when undertaking an examination of any system’s power system. Load flow analysis reveals the voltage of the entire system, the quantity of active and reactive power generated and consumed, and the line losses. By doing a short circuit analysis, you may assess if your power system can manage the potential of power component failure. 

A short circuit may have serious effects. It might result in overheating, a fire, or even an explosion if the circuit is damaged. Short circuits and load flow can severely reduce the efficiency of a machine. This blog article explains in detail how to discover problems such as load flow and short circuits using simulation approaches. We use specialized software referred to as the Electrical Transient Analyzer Program to investigate load flow and short circuit issues (ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program)). Using ETAP data, a comprehensive analysis of load flow and short circuit was done. 

Why are short circuit and load flow studies so important for Hong Kong businesses? 

In every business, the current electrical infrastructure is problematic. Short circuits and load flow were the two most common problems. A load flow and short circuit study is the best technique to determine what is causing these load flow and short circuit issues. 

Table of Contents
Why Does a Short Circuit Occur?
A broken wire or insufficient insulation may result in a short circuit or energy surge if current passes via the neutral line.
Poor connection A circuit requires a solid connection. Allowing electricity to flow via the neutral or grounded conductor might produce a short circuit if a wire is loose.
Use a damaged outlet: If anything is connected to a damaged outlet, short circuits may develop.

In contrast, a transient electrical system surge typically causes load flow. Load flow analysis is a technique used by professionals at CareLabs to solve problems, particularly those involving load flow. Short circuit analysis resolves difficulties with short circuits. CareLabs has specialists that can do load flow and short circuit analysis to determine what is wrong and assist you in fixing it.

Working Principles for Power Quality in Hong Kong

The quality of the power supply has a direct impact on the performance of the equipment connected to the distribution network. It is anticipated that the equipment will function as intended and have a useful life equal to that of the service given. Similarly, it is envisaged that the network would be reliable enough to avoid issues such as substandard output or functional loss. The power grid has a great deal of issues. Regardless of how long they have existed, these power quality issues can cause equipment damage. Optimal power quality must constantly be maintained to prevent device malfunctions. This blog will discuss the research required to evaluate the quality and reliability of power. The subsequent step is the examination of alternative power quality systems and ways for evaluating the effect and the control.

Unlike the effects of dependability, which pertain to lengthy power outages, the effects of power quality disruptions are not immediately obvious. Even though an interruption in power quality is sometimes accompanied by broken equipment or a delay in a process, the impacts are felt immediately. This is especially true for short-duration noises such as sags and pauses. In some circumstances, it may take time for the impacts of a change in power quality to become apparent. Failure of equipment is one example. In this situation, nobody observes the progressive depreciation of the equipment. Other times, power quality problems will lead to extra losses that are absorbed as a standard cost of doing business and are not reported. 

Table of Contents

What Happens When the Electricity Goes Out?

The following are immediate economic effects:
  • The decline in output
  • Restarting a procedure incurs additional expenses.
  • Costs of equipment damage repair and replacement
  • Even more pauses and restarts
  • People are concerned for their safety and health.
  • Costs incurred because of a contract violation
  • Environmental protection violations are punishable.
  • The disruption caused an increase in utility expenses.
Listed below are some indirect economic effects:
  • The expenses incurred by a company due to a delay in sales or earnings.
  • How much does a decline in market share cost?
  • How much does it cost to rebuild brand value?
Among these socioeconomic implications are:
  • An increase in temperature within a building poses a threat to people’s health, safety, or performance.
  • An issue or harm to the individual
  • If the change in power quality goes unnoticed, electrical risk might follow.

Identifying the Fundamental Reasons of Insufficient Power:

Voltage, frequency, and waveform of a power supply system disclose whether or not it meets power quality criteria. However, a variety of factors influence the quality of power. The following issues with electricity quality are discussed in this blog: 

  1. Continuous current
  2. Inadequate voltage
  3. Electromagnetic radiation
  4. Flashing or fluctuating voltage
  5. Power outages and brownouts

Less study has been conducted on phenomena that occur less often, such as noise, waves, and transients. 

1. Continuous current:

Stable-state voltages are those that remain consistent over an extended time (minutes or hours). Long-term high voltage levels (overvoltage) or low voltage levels (undervoltage) have a range of detrimental impacts on equipment, including equipment failure, increased energy consumption, and potential system failure.  

2. Inadequate voltage:

Unbalanced voltage typically causes increased losses, particularly in induction and three-phase motors.

  • As a result, the bearings deteriorate, and an inverted torque is produced.
  • Increased heating of the stator and rotor
  • Installation cables may transport less current.
  • Neutral conductors have more losses.
  • Greater energy loss occurs in cables.
3. Electromagnetic radiation

Most energy users produce harmonic currents that are fed into the power grid. Alternating currents with harmonic frequencies have multiples of 50 hertz, which is typically referred to as the fundamental frequency. Computers, variable speed drives (VSD), and discharge lamps are examples of nonlinear electrical equipment that frequently produce harmonic currents. Harmonic currents have the potential to seriously harm both the electrical system and the installation. Components, installation parts, component loading, misusing the installation’s present carrying capacity, shortened component lifespan, and ineffective protective component operation are only a few of the things that harmonic might harm.

4. Flashing or fluctuating voltage

Changing the envelope of a voltage waveform causes flickering. It results in a consistent change in the luminosity of incandescent lights. This frequent change in the quantity of light emitted may cause headaches, decrease productivity, and, in rare cases, induce seizures in certain individuals. 

5. Power outages and brownouts

Most equipment is unaffected by voltage fluctuations and interruptions, but extended interruptions can cause equipment to trip and lose output. A generalization that may be made is that the cost of a protracted interruption is not proportionate to its length. Instead, it follows a logarithmic curve. People assert that the following variables have a substantial influence on the cost of a prolonged disruption:

  • In the manufacturing industry, for instance, the cost difference between a one-second, one-minute, and one-hour disruption is reported to be little.
  • The event’s date, time, and place (weekday, weekend, daytime, evening) (weekday, weekend, daytime, nighttime)

AEMO uses the value of customer reliability (VCR) to determine how much interruptions cost all users. Even though these numbers mostly indicate how much customers are ready to pay to avoid an interruption, this desire to pay is tightly correlated with the actual cost. According to these estimates, a one-second outage costs around 20% less than an hour-long interruption. 

How Can Power Quality Problems Be Kept to a Minimum?

CareLabs offers several services to discover and evaluate power quality concerns. They are listed below:

  • Load Flow Analysis: Depending on how the system is utilized, this analysis is conducted. It predicts power flow, voltage, power factor, and system losses.
  • Using computer algorithms, harmonic analysis is the process of searching for and forecasting probable harmonic difficulties. Depending on the findings, reduction methods are also recommended.
  • Analysis of Surges and Transients: This study examines the causes of surges and transients.
  • This study would track the voltage’s short-term drops and climbs, as well as its value and trajectory.
  • This study of reactive power will find the ideal reactive power at the distribution and load ends.
  • This research would assess how much captive power is necessary to meet demand while minimizing energy surcharges and fuel use.

Using the ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software, CareLabs investigates and evaluates power quality. It is the best program for calculating and modelling all the components of an electrical system to guarantee the safety of the equipment. We provide power quality analysis services in major cities including Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau Island CareLabs tries to give you the best service possible to improve the quality and dependability of your electrical installation. Contact us immediately to schedule a power quality study or to receive a quote.
